entry.title: Re: Field Values in Intelligent Event Processing custom e...
entry.content: But what about the other field, "Event Context"? I cannot find any help on that field at all.

entry.title: How to force password reset?
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

How to force a user to reset their password?

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to force password reset?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

SAP Customer Data Cloud supports an operation that allows a site's administrator to manually expire a user password such that the user will be prompted to set a new password upon their next login. This can be done by calling the "accounts.setAccountInfo" REST API method and passing the "requirePasswordChange" parameter as "true".

Please note the following:

  • it is necessary to call this for each user that you want to manually expire their password
  • it is important to ensure that a "data-on-pending-password-change-screen" is correctly mapped on the login screen-set so that the user will be able to complete this flow (in the UI Builder the default screen used for this is the "Mandatory Pwd Change" screen)

 

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: Re: How do I get an item in the recycle bin to be "Deleted"?
entry.content:
Following an upgrade, we were able to delete the offending file.  Not sure why the upgrade helped; but it allowed the deletion to occur. 

 

We went to 4.3 SP4 P800

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Destination to s4 Public Cloud Gives error wh...
entry.content: But the process to create the BP is started/triggered in the S4 correct? So you might want to implement an event to be capture by the SAP Build Process Automation tool to execute the conditions check. Or how will you trigger the API READ in S4? Is it S4 Public Cloud or OnPrem or Private?

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ Training by SAP
entry.content:
Hi @chakrad71 
Here are the some material we currently provide on SAP learning related to this topic:

  •  Implementing SAP CPQ course
    This course is designed to grant you a fundamental understanding of SAP CPQ concepts, features, and functionality.
  • Discovering the capabilities of SAP Solutions for Quote-to-Cash Management private cloud (BRIM) for Solution Architects
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Contract Accounting & Invoicing
  • Discovering the capabilities of SAP Solutions for Quote-to-Cash Management private cloud (BRIM) for Business User

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: E-Mail error no delivery to test@test-com
entry.content:
What is the cause of an email output error "No delivery to test@test-com"


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: E-Mail error no delivery to test@test-com
entry.content:
Why do we do get this error?

An email address like test@test-com is not valid according to standard email formatting rules.

✅ Valid Email Format (RFC 5322 Standard)

A valid email address must follow this structure:

local-part@domain

Where:

  • local-part: Can include letters, numbers, and some special characters.
  • domain: Must include at least one dot (.) to separate the domain name and the top-level domain (TLD), like .com, .org, .net, etc.

❌ Why test@test-com is Invalid

  • The domain part test-com is missing a dot.
  • It should be something like test@test.com or test@sub.test.com.

✅ Valid Examples

  • test@example.com
  • user.name@company.co.uk
  • admin@sub.domain.org

entry.title: Re: Non Standard Allocation in SAC
entry.content: Yeah, SAC is really not good enough for such N:N relationships. You are forced into restricted workarounds or remodeling of your plan data in unnatural ways.

Can we use WAIT UP TO 2 SECONDS. after calling BAPI_TRASACTION_COMMIT will it fix the issue?

Option 2: I am checking this option.

entry.title: Re: Connect with SAP Community at SAP Sapphire and Share Your Special SAP Event Moment!
entry.content: Sapphire offers so much and everyone has fun! It is wonderful to see such a fantastic line up of Sapphire speakers again this year @moshenaveh. Never a dull moment at events with our community members as well as community leaders (#SAPMentors and #SAPChampions.) @boobboo @DiegoDora @HenrikWagner ...and so many more! I'm looking forward to meeting community members as well as reading everyone's key-takeaways/next steps in their SAP journey in the SAP Community! PS Everyone should consider being a (local) event organizer too! Become an Event Organizer | SAP Community @lauraschmitz @KatherineKenneally

entry.title: Re: How to improve Date Dimension labels in a line chart...
entry.content: Hello, I have been using these Time dimensions in Datasphere to create associations with dates from my datasets, so that I can finally use date hierarchies in SAC - but this still does not allow me to display month names, only month numbers. I also tried to bring the time dimension (time dimension - day specifically by outer joining it to my dataset) but even if i use this date in SAC, I still only have months as numbers in the bar chart. No month names. Can you elaborate if there is a specific way to enable the display of month names e.g. in a bar chart?

entry.title: Re: Combine cube's LEFT join returns no data when right s...
entry.content:
Known Bug: for 4.3 SP4 P800.

See KBA: 3553252 https://me.sap.com/notes/3553252/E.

Document indicates patched in Version 4.3 4 P1100

entry.title: Re: posting period - M7053
entry.content:
Seems the system didn't allow you to skip so many periods, did you get any error,

  • Look at acua current MM period with MMRV
  • Look at current period status wih MMPV using 'check only' and today date
  • Try to close every period one per one (hoping MMPV skip many periods is not active)
  • Check also if restrictions related to user parameters MMPV_READ_NOTE and MPV_NUMBER_DAYS_ALLOWED (Note 1082841 ) are active
  • In test systems, consider scheduing report RMMMPERI in a periodic job (with dynamic current date in the variant)

entry.title: Re: Edit Time Valuation Result
entry.content:
Hi Laura,

As far as I know, this is not possible. Even if it is, I would highly recommend a closer look at this process. What you are describing here would likely not be legal, at least here in the US. In the old SAP CATS, where only recorded time was approved, many customers asked that the evaluated time be included in the approval. However, the manager really should just be approving that the time entered is correct. If they are making a decision that one person gets overtime, but another does not for the same time entry, then that is unfair to the employees. 

Also, how would you reconcile this? Two employees with the same time entry could have different results. If you are trying to troubleshoot this and explain to the employee, then you have this extra random decision being made.

So, if there is some difference between the overtime that they want to approve and the overtime that they don't want to approve, then the best practice would be to record something different at the source (the time sheet).

entry.title: Re: S4HANA 1909 to 2303 upgrade - SAP FI-CA new functionn...

On the other side, if I search in the same "Oil, Gas and Energy" Industries with keyword "FICA" I get less results but I would avoid skipping some important informations ;  I wonder if you can help me further to focus on modules ?

Thanks for your time.

Have a great day.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Destination to s4 Public Cloud Gives error wh...
entry.content: @TiagoRibeiro S4 Public Cloud. Yes the trigger will be a API Event BP create in S4 Public cloud and on that trigger in BPA we get the BP ID and we use this action to read details of BP from S4 to validate some fields.

entry.title: Re: how do you set up Time sheet and Time Recording profile together in time management?
entry.content: Can you give some more details on your question? The Time Recording Profile is a configuration item that is part of setting up the Time Sheet. This should be covered in the Implementation Guide.

entry.title: Re: Me22n IDOC_INPUT_ORDERS
entry.content: It's a different IDoc Process Code to configure in WE20: ME10 for ME21N and ME11 for ME22N. Source: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/configuration-to-send-po-idocs/qaq-p/8544744.

entry.title: Migrate Outbound B2B ASCX12 interfaces from SAP PI/PO to CI(CPI) using BIMA Package
entry.content:
Many customers use SAP PI/PO systems for B2B transactions that use ASC X12 message types. When they decide to migrate to SAP Cloud Integration, they encounter some challenges. Specifically, SAP PI/PO uses XML Schema Definition (XSD) format that aren't compatible with Cloud Integration - XML to EDI or EDI to XML converters, making it difficult to re-use existing SAP PI/PO mappings in Cloud Integration.

To solve this problem, we have created a package called the B2B Interface Migration Accelerator – ASCX12 which is in SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This package helps move your B2B ASCX12 interfaces to the cloud easily, saving time and effort by using your existing SAP PI/PO message mappings.

 

1.  Concept:

Whenever xml file which is PI/PO xsd specific or Cloud Integration xsd specific enters Cloud Integration iFlow, the structure is modified with the help of XSLT mapping based on the reference xml file which is stored in the Partner directory. This also depends on the flow. When the files are triggered from Company to the Trading Partner then it is Outbound Flow. When the files are triggered from Trading Partner to the Company then it is Inbound Flow. 

In this blog, we'll explore at how to migrate outbound B2B interfaces that use ASCX12 message types.

For migration ASCX12 inbound interfaces, please refer to blog

2.  High-Level Design – Outbound Flow

In an outbound flow, a file triggered from the Company, enters a specific iFlow in Cloud Integration. Then, a generic iFlow is called which compares the structure of this file with a reference XML file stored in the Partner directory. After making necessary changes, the revised file is sent to the Trading Partner. This setup ensures the file is in the right format for the Trading Partner.

In the above depiction,

  1. XML file is received by the iFlow outbound template (specific iFlow)
  2. XML file sent to existing message mapping which is imported from SAP PI/PO
  3. Check whether standard solution to support PI/PO specific xsd file in the converter is available
  4. If not, call the XML structure modifier flow (Generic iFlow)
  5. Modified XML file from Generic iFlow is sent to XML-EDI converter
  6. Output is sent to Trading Partner

3.  Migration Steps – Outbound Flow

Example: Migrate an outbound ASCX12 interface which has message type, 810 and version, 004010 from SAP PI/PO to CI

Preparation:

Refer the Configuration Guide maintained in the Document section of this package, complete Pre-requisites and copy the package to your workspace/tenant.

1. Go to Integrations under Discover, select the package and copy it to your tenant

2. Refer Configuration Guide maintained in the Document Section

3. From the package, copy the outbound template flow to a separate package which is Trading Partner specific

4. To create a reference file for outbound flow of message type 810 and version 004010, Go to Type Systems, Select ASC X12, Messages 810 and version 004010

Click on Create MIG, Select with Envelope

Upload a sample EDI flat file which contains all the segments and fields required by all the Trading Partners for ASC 810 004010 integration

Select the Nodes based on the payload, Click on Next, provide required name, direction as outbound and Create Invoice as Business Process under Own Business Context

Click on Create and this created MIG can be exported as SAP Cloud Integration Runtime Artifacts

In this exported artifact, we would need the below highlighted ones. Xml file to upload as reference xml file in the Partner Directory and xsd to upload in the XML to EDI Converter

5. Create Partner Directories with names mentioned in the Configuration Guide

I have uploaded test data from above downloaded artifact  which has all the fields utilized by all the partners in the landscape for 810 message type and 04010 message version. Hence, this payload can be the reference xml file and it is sufficient for me to migrate all the 810 message type interfaces for all the Trading Partners.

Configuration:

1. In this Trading Partner Specific Outbound flow, I will configure the sender and receiver systems

2. Under Mapping Flow, I will configure the existing message mapping for this Trading Partner which is imported from SAP PI/PO

3. In the XML to EDI converter under converter flow, upload Cloud Integration specific ASC 810 message type of version 004010 xsd from the downloaded artificat which was exported from Integration Advisor in step#4 of Preparation

4. Deploy the following Artifacts

Testing:

I have completed the configuration and in my outbound flow Company is sending an INVOIC IDOC which needs to be converted to EDI format before sending it to Trading Partner

Triggered INVOIC IDOC should be passed through the imported message mapping from SAP PI/PO

Outbound INVOIC IDOC from the company:      

Output of the message mapping which EDI XML file which is in PI/PO specific structure:

This file can’t be passed directly to XML to EDI Converter because the converter uses CI specific xsd for conversion. Hence the Structure Modifier Generic flow is called which will modify this PI/PO specific structure to CI specific structure by referring to the reference file uploaded in the Partner Directory.

Output of XML Structure Modifier Flow:

This modified file can be sent to XML to EDI converter and the output can be forwarded to Trading Partner

Out EDI file sent to Trading Partner:

Status in the Message Monitor:

4.  Summary – Outbound Flow

Migrating outbound B2B interfaces using ASC X12 message types to SAP Cloud Integration is a structured process that effectively utilizes existing SAP PI/PO mappings. The outbound template plays a central role, enabling file structure transformation through specific and generic iFlows to meet Trading Partner needs. Furthermore, the outbound template is flexible, allowing for extensions in mapping logic and the incorporation of a retry mechanism through the exception flow, catering to varied business requirements. Once the standard solution is available by SAP to support SAP PI/PO xsds in the converters, structure modification flow can be easily switched off in the Decider Flow.

A key aspect of this migration is the creation and maintenance of Partner directories and reference XML files. This setup is a one-time task that significantly simplifies ongoing operations. For instance, if your business needs outbound interfaces for ASC X12 message type 810, version 004010 across 50 Trading Partners, you only need to maintain one reference XML file with all necessary segments for these 50 partners. This reference file serves as a benchmark, allowing inbound XML files to be compared and altered accordingly.

The Partner directory SAP_PI_to_CPI_Outbound is created once and used universally for all outbound flows and message versions. For example, If there is a Purchase order change outbound interface which used message type 860 with version 004010 then the reference file for this flow is added in the same Partner director,  SAP_PI_to_CPI_Outbound without further setup.

This approach streamlines the migration process, reduces redundancy, and ensures efficient modification and management of XML structures, making it a cost-effective solution for transitioning to Cloud Integration  while maintaining reliable communication with Trading Partners

5.  Additional Read

Migration Guide : https://help.sap.com/docs/migration-guide-po/migration-guide-for-sap-process-orchestration/interface-migration?locale=en-US&q=message+mapping

Feel free to share your thoughts and experiences in the comments section below. Whether you have questions about the migration process, insights from your own experiences, or suggestions for further improvements, we'd love to hear from you. Your feedback can help enhance understanding and provide valuable knowledge to others navigating similar transitions.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Destination to s4 Public Cloud Gives error wh...
entry.content: So the current issue you are facing is while receiving the event when the BP is created, meaning you don't receive the event, or when you execute the ODATA Read action to the BP communication arrangement in S4?

entry.title: Migrate Inbound B2B ASCX12 interfaces from SAP PI/PO to CI(CPI) using BIMA Package
entry.content:
Many customers use SAP PI/PO systems for B2B transactions that use ASC X12 message types. When they decide to migrate to SAP Cloud Integration, they encounter some challenges. Specifically, SAP PI/PO uses XML Schema Definition (XSD) format that aren't compatible with Cloud Integration - XML to EDI or EDI to XML converters, making it difficult to re-use existing SAP PI/PO mappings in Cloud Integration.

To solve this problem, we have created a package called the B2B Interface Migration Accelerator – ASCX12 which is in SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This package helps move your B2B ASCX12 interfaces to the cloud easily, saving time and effort by using your existing SAP PI/PO message mappings.

 

 

1.  Concept:    

Whenever xml file which is PI/PO xsd specific or Cloud Integration xsd specific enters Cloud Integration iflow, the structure is modified with the help of XSLT mapping based on the reference xml file which is stored in the Partner directory. This also depends on the flow. When the files are triggered from Company to the Trading Partner then it is Outbound Flow. When the files are triggered from Trading Partner to the Company then it is Inbound Flow.

In this blog, we'll explore at how to migrate inbound B2B interfaces that use ASC X12 message types.

For migrating ASCX12 outbound interfaces, please refer to blog

2.  High-Level Design – Inbound Flow

In an inbound flow, a file triggered from the Trading Partner enters a specific iFlow in Cloud Integration. Then, a generic iFlow is called which compares the structure of this inbound file with a reference XML file stored in the Partner directory. After making necessary changes, the revised file is sent to the Company. This setup ensures the file is in the right format for the Company.

In the above depiction,

  1. EDI file is received by the iFlow inbound template (specific iFlow)
  2. EDI  file sent to EDI to XML Converter and the output of this is EDI XML file is in CI specific XML format because there is no standard support for SAP PI/PO specific xsd in the converter
  3. Call the XML structure modifier flow (Generic iFlow)
  4. Modified XML file from Generic iFlow is sent to Mapping flow
  5. Output is sent to Company

3.  Migration Steps – Inbound Flow

Example: Migrate an inbound ASCX12 interface which has message type, 850 and version, 004010 from SAP PI/PO to CI

Preparation:

Refer the Configuration Guide maintained in the Document section of this package, complete Pre-requisites and copy the package to your workspace/tenant.

1. Go to Integrations under Discover, select the package and copy it to your tenan

2. Refer Configuration Guide maintained in the Document Section

3. From the package, copy the inbound template flow to a separate package which is Trading Partner specific

4. To create a reference file for inbound flow of message type 850 and version 004010, Go to Type Systems, Select ASC X12, Messages 850 and version 004010

Click on Export XSD

5. Create Partner Directories with names mentioned in the Configuration Guide.

I have uploaded a sample EDI XML file as value from SAP PI/PO which is in SAP PI/PO specific format and has all the fields utilized by all the partners in the landscape for 850 message type and 04010 message version. Hence, this payload can be the reference xml file and it is sufficient for me to migrate all the 850 message type interfaces for all the Trading Partners.

Configuration:

1. In this Trading Partner Specific Inbound flow, I will configure the sender and receiver systems

2. Under Mapping Flow, configure the existing message mapping for this Trading Partner which is imported from SAP PI/PO

3. In the EDI to XML converter under converter flow, upload Cloud Integration specific ASC 850 message type of version 004010 xsd from the downloaded artifact which was exported from Integration Advisor in step#4 of Preparation

4. Add required namespace URI and namespace prefix. By default it is saved as #NOT_USED# but if your wsdl/xsd in the SAP PI/PO imported message mapping needs it then it should be configured.

In my case, my wsdl which is used in the SAP PI/PO imported message mapping or this trading partner uses prefix as ns0 and namespace URI as http://sap.com/xi/B2B/Example

5. Deploy the following Artifacts

Testing:

I have completed the configuration and in my inbound flow Trading Partner is sending ASC 850 EDI file which needs to be converted to ORDERS IDOC format before sending it to Company

Triggered 850 EDI file will be passed through converter flow where EDI file is converted to EDI xml file which CI specific structure. Since this structure can’t be passed directly to imported SAP PI/PO message mapping which is expecting the file to be in PI/PO specific structure, we will call the generic flow which will do the structure modification and convert CI specific EDI xml file to PI/PO specific EDI xml file

Inbound 850 EDI file from the Trading Partner:

Output of the converter which is a EDI XML file CI specific structure:

This file can’t be passed directly to SAP PI/PO imported message mapping. Hence the Structure Modifier Generic flow is called which will modify this CI specific structure to PI/PO specific EDI XML structure by referring to the reference file uploaded in the Partner Directory.

Output of XML Structure Modifier Flow:

This modified file can be sent to SAP PI/PO imported message mapping and the output can be forwarded to the Company

Output of the SAP PI/PO message mapping which can be sent to Company:

Status in the Message Monitor:

4.  Summary – Inbound Flow

Migrating inbound B2B interfaces using ASC X12 message types to SAP Cloud Integration is a well-organized task that makes use of existing SAP PI/PO mappings. The key is the inbound template, which transforms the file structures through specific and generic iFlows so they fit correctly into the company's systems. This template is flexible, so you can adjust it to add additional mapping logic or handle retries if errors occur. Once the standard solution is available by SAP to support SAP PI/PO xsds in the converters, structure modification flow can be easily switched off in the Converter Flow.

One crucial step in the migration process is setting up Partner directories and reference XML files. This is done once and greatly simplifies future operations. For example, if your company is handling inbound interfaces for ASC X12 message type 850, version 004010 for several Trading Partners, you only need to create one reference XML file that includes all necessary segments. This single reference file can be used to adjust incoming XML files, ensuring they fit the SAP PI/PO format.

The Partner directory, named SAP_PI_to_CPI_Inbound, is created once and can be used for all inbound flows and message versions. If a new inbound flow, like sales order processing using message type 850 with version 004010, is added, its reference file can be included in this directory without needing further setup. Using these directories and reference files reduces repetition, makes XML structure management straightforward, and offers a resource-efficient way to shift to Cloud Integration while keeping communication with Trading Partners smooth and reliable.

5.  Additional Read

Migration Guide : https://help.sap.com/docs/migration-guide-po/migration-guide-for-sap-process-orchestration/interface-migration?locale=en-US&q=message+mapping

We'd love to hear from you in the comments section below. Feel free to share your questions about the migration process, your own experiences, or any suggestions for improvement. Your feedback is valuable, helping others who are also navigating similar transitions and contributing to a shared pool of practical knowledge.

entry.title: Spool Request with BIN Output in Landscape – Always Prints in Portrait
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working on generating a spool request with binary (BIN) content, where the actual content is formatted in landscape orientation. However, the print output always comes out in portrait, despite the data being prepared correctly. As a result, only half of the text is visible, and the rest is cut off or shifted, since the orientation doesn’t match the data layout.

Here’s the code I’m using to create the spool request:

CALL FUNCTION 'RSPO_SR_OPEN'
EXPORTING
dest = CONV rspopname( lv_ldest ) 
layout = 'G_RAW'
doctype = 'BIN'
titleline = lv_title
suffix1 = lv_suffix1
suffix2 = lv_suffix2
name = lv_name
immediate_print = abap_true
copies = lv_copies
IMPORTING
handle = lv_spool_handle
spoolid = lv_spool
EXCEPTIONS
device_missing = 1
name_twice = 2
no_such_device = 3
operation_failed = 4
OTHERS = 5.

Does anyone know how to force the output to print in landscape orientation?
Is there a specific setting I need to adjust in the layout parameter, or maybe in the printer device configuration (e.g., LOCL)?
Could it be a limitation of the 'BIN' document type?

Any tips or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Different document type for SAP EWM and 3rd party warehouse for PO and INB
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working on SAP S4HANA, we have 2 different warehouse systems doing goods receipt. One is SAP EWM and one is a 3rd party system. Process from creating a PO till an inbound delivery is same.

Shall I create a different PO and inbound delivery type for process differenciation? 

or what is recommended as I want to perform a setup.

Thank you!

Regards Dominik

entry.title: Re: Field Values in Intelligent Event Processing custom e...

entry.title: GL Accounts are not copied to Quality/Test system
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have added new company code in CBC, and in customizing all GL accounts are copied to new company code.

Moved the TR to Quality system but not all GL accounts are copied

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: GL Accounts are not copied to Quality/Test system
entry.content:
See help.sap.com documentation 

Implementation Status

See referenced therein

"All accounts created by content activation, such as initial implementation, country/region activation, or scope item activation, are by default marked I. With this feature, you can choose to set these accounts to either N or P, or leave them in status I.

Accounts with status N or I remain in your lowest system and are not available in your higher systems.

In a two-system landscape, this means that those accounts marked N or I are only available in your quality system.

In a three-system landscape, this means that those accounts marked N or I are only available in your development system."

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Service issue in Business Application Studio and Visual Studio Code to ABAP Trail
entry.content:
Hi all,

I created a new BTP trail access last week. Then I created an ABAp Trail using a booster. Then also created apps using Booster SAP Buils. Everything was created without errors. When I call the connection in the BAS in the Service Center, I get the following error: 

The same occurs in the Visual Studio:

The Connection Check in BTP is ok.

Does anyone have this problem. If so, how was it solved?

Best Regards

Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP APIM external Logging using New Relic
entry.content:
Hi @Muniyappan ,

I have a similar requirement which is to send logs to Splunk in case of errors in SAP APIM. We have have a set of 5 policies in ProxyEndPoint - Postflow which generates a message structure and send it to Splunk. But it only sends the API response for successful calls as these policies never gets executed if an error occur anywhere in the Proxy object. 

Following your blog I tried including the faultRules without a condition and have referenced all 5 policies so that errors are also sent to Splunk. But I get the following error while trying to import the API as a zipped after editing the default.xml files:
Error:

Unable to create API Proxy; Contents in the zip file is invalid.Unable to create API Proxy; Contents in the zip file is invalid. Verify the directory structure inside the zip.
Here is how the files looks like:
 
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ProxyEndPoint default="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
	<name>default</name>
	<base_path>/ECCompoundEmployee/dynamic</base_path>
	<properties/>
	<routeRules>
		<routeRule>
			<name>default</name>
			<targetEndPointName>default</targetEndPointName>
			<sequence>1</sequence>
			<faultRules/>
		</routeRule>
	</routeRules>
	<faultRules>
		<faultRule>
			<name>LogErrors</name>
			<condition/>
			<steps>
				<step>
					<policy_name>KVMO_CPICredentials</policy_name>
					<condition/>
					<sequence>1</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>KVMO_GetCPIUrl</policy_name>
					<condition/>
					<sequence>2</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>LoggingBasicAuth</policy_name>
					<condition/>
					<sequence>3</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>generateLogMessage</policy_name>
					<condition/>
					<sequence>4</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>logToElasticSearch</policy_name>
					<condition/>
					<sequence>5</sequence>
				</step>
			</steps>
		</faultRule>
	</faultRules>
	<preFlow>
		<name>PreFlow</name>
		<request>
			<isRequest>true</isRequest>
			<steps>
				<step>
					<policy_name>XMLThreatProtection</policy_name>
					<condition> </condition>
					<sequence>3</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>JSONThreatProtection</policy_name>
					<condition> </condition>
					<sequence>2</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>VerifyAPIKey</policy_name>
					<condition> </condition>
					<sequence>1</sequence>
				</step>
			</steps>
		</request>
		<response>
			<isRequest>true</isRequest>
			<steps/>
		</response>
	</preFlow>
	<postFlow>
		<name>PostFlow</name>
		<request>
			<isRequest>true</isRequest>
			<steps/>
		</request>
		<response>
			<isRequest>true</isRequest>
			<steps>
				<step>
					<policy_name>KVMO_GetCPIUrl</policy_name>
					<condition> </condition>
					<sequence>2</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>KVMO_CPICredentials</policy_name>
					<condition> </condition>
					<sequence>1</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>generateLogMessage</policy_name>
					<condition> </condition>
					<sequence>3</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>LoggingBasicAuth</policy_name>
					<condition> </condition>
					<sequence>4</sequence>
				</step>
				<step>
					<policy_name>logToElasticSearch</policy_name>
					<condition> </condition>
					<sequence>5</sequence>
				</step>
			</steps>
		</response>
	</postFlow>
	<conditionalFlows/>
</ProxyEndPoint>

Any suggestions on what I might be doing wrong? Thanks in advance!

Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: Re: SAP APIM external Logging using New Relic
entry.content:
Hi Faisal, 

This is a common error and I have faced this with mac.If you using mac try os command. 

Before making any changes, try this method:
Download the ZIP file and extract it. Dont make any changes. just compress the extracted contents into a new ZIP file and upload it again. If you are able to succeed, then proceed with changes.

APIProxy should be the  root folder inside zip file. can you show your proxy folder?

entry.title: Re: Advance Return Management complete configuration(SAP ARM)
entry.content:
Hello @surykumar158 , 

first of all, thank you very much for your detailed documentation. 

We are also currently working with extended returns and so far I have been able to set everything up so that an inbound delivery is created, I post at first to blocked stock and an inspection is carried out, in which I then selected the follow-up action 0011 Transfer to Free Available Stock and completed the inspection. Then I have created the corresponding credit memo request and invoiced it. The problem now, however, is the posting to the freely usable stock when its first in blocked stock.

We get the error "Interval / does not exist for object RV_BELEG". I think that the system did not find any range,...

 

,.. but all documents have a number range added

DocumentNumber range
RE207
LR213
G212

Here is the booking into blocked stock. We use LE-WM. 

Do you have any idea where the number range should also be maintained? Is there any outbound delivery that the system is not trying to create here?

 

When I try to book directly into free stock it works without problems.

Many thanks in advance 🙂

Christian

entry.title: Re: Edit Time Valuation Result
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the reply. If overtime hours were recorded manually, it would be easy to approve or reject these requests individually. In this specific case, there is no manual process, it is all integrated. So assuming an employee works 3 hours of overtime that were not pre-approved, how do we safeguard the approver's decision? or even, if a manager knows that the employee was not actually working despite having done the clocking, how does he decide that he wants to pay X hours of those recorded overtime hours? In this case, it is incorrect to delete the records but there should be a mechanism that overrides this..

entry.title: How can you create a Story Report that shows who has and doesn't have individual goals?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am fairly new to report story and after covering a lot of the community I am a little stuck on creating an effective report that would show readers the number of employees that have and don't have Goals. 

Looking over a lot of other posts I have tried to follow creating the below Query:

And then creating a Calculated Column in the Query:

However, I can see that in the data source it doesn't seem to be picking up the employees that have no objective and thus is only returning those that have a value/goal.

Can you advise maybe where I am going wrong?

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: How to Convert Retail Site Master from SAP ERP to S/4HANA - Updated
entry.content:
Hi! We are currently preparing a ECC to S4 conversion, and the consistency check returns error on BP role assignment for the retail sites.

As john1v comments above, it is not all clear to us if we need to create the BP roles with category BPSITE in ECC pre-conversion, or if this is handled by the migration program. Do you have any insights?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Create Sales Order with Billing Plan through API in S...
entry.content: As a half solution, SD consultant have created a Standard Billing Plan on SAP and we reference it in API_SALES_ORDER_SRV field A_SalesOrderItemType/BillingPlan. This way it's created according to the configuration

entry.title: SuccessFactors AI - AI-Assisted Performance Insights for Manager (Set up and hints)
entry.content:
Leveraging generative AI capabilities, managers can now gain insights into employees' performance in the latest experience of Performance Management forms.

With proper configurations, managers in the single-role step can access the feature by selecting the Generate button in a new area called Boost your review with AI-powered insights! at the beginning of the form

 

To get the AI-Assisted Performance Insights tool activated some small steps are needed.

1. Fullfill the pre-requisites: you have to have 

  • licenses for AI Units
  • you have enabled the latest experience of Performance Management forms via Admin Center >> Performance Management Feature Settings 
  • you have enabled Assisted Performance Insights for Manager Review via Admin Center >> Form Template Settings >> <your forms>

2. Activate the function

With the AI Units you will find a new admin page in SuccessFactors where you can activate AI Scenarios (AI Service Administration). Grant the permissions to this page to your user to manage the AI Scenarios.

 

In this application (AI Service Administration) you have to activate Assisted Performance Insights. With this setting you have activated the function in overall.

 

 

3. Assign permissions to your users

To make sure users can use the function add the scenarios to the relevant permission roles.

With activation of AI functions we have a new RBP category available under user permissions -> AI Access

Select the "Assisted Performance Insights" permissions [should be a manager permission].

 

DONE 

How to find the insights button:

Open the form in a relevant manager status:

You can generate the insight on the top of the new forms UI.

 

Important note about the data that is used to generate the content:

We are using data about the employee's performance that is created between the form start date and due date. The data contains:

  • Feedback, achievements, and meeting notes from Continuous Performance Management
  • Comments from Performance Management forms
  • Feedback provided through Ask for Feedback in Performance Management

 

With the new UI and the activation of assisted writing you have also the option to get support during comment generation. More details here: SuccessFactors AI - AI-Assisted Successor Insights... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Can we disable "Training Overdue Send Reminder" Popup...
entry.content:
@Michelle_Smithard, removing the "Manager Reminders" permission will lead to the removal of the "Send Reminder" from the Learning home page as well. Allowing the edition of emails via the Learning home page doesn't stand to be planned. This is a recent enhancement asking for it, which has been updated by the Learning Product Management team stating that it'd violate design rules.

@CarrieMason, the "Manager Reminders" permission is found in the user's role > "My Team" block (Team View and Overdue Training Reminders).

entry.title: Re: DMEEX structure supporting excel file S4 Hana
entry.content:
Dear,

Please, check SAP Notes 2820775 - Source of information for field in structures FPAYH-FPAYHX-FPAYP. 

This note has some attached file with the structure and probably it may help you.

Simone Brito

entry.title: Re: MLT goal rating that exceeds 5 point scale not displa...
entry.content: Solution: MLT scale in goal plan should be adjusted to accommodate ratings over 5

entry.title: SAP TPI counterparty to BP
entry.content:
Hi 

We have an issue in the mapping of Counterparty in TPI.

The scenario is we have 360t, and they have only one provider "jp morgan" to create differents trades in differents company codes; so when in 360t create a deal with provider "jp morgan" for company code 0055 the BP is T-0055 and when the deal is for company code 0066 the BP is T-0066. 

I did not find the way to create this mapping because I did not find the rule to add company code in Countertary app.

Is that possible? 

Provider: jp morgan

BP: depend the company code (T-0055 or T-0066)

thanks for your guidance

Best 

GD

entry.title: Re: How to Maintain Exchange Rates in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hi, this doesn't cover the Concur to S/4HANA public cloud currency integration provided by ICS.  Does anyone know how this works and what it does ?  many thanks

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2025 in Orlando -top picks for SAP Service Cloud
entry.content:
Hear inspiring customer stories, learn about AI agents and discover the benefits of connected, end to end customer service. Check out the list of can’t-miss SAP Service Cloud sessions in SAP Sapphire Orlando:

Bosch Power Tools' success in accelerating customer service with AI - CXP2792
Wednesday, May 21 | 11:30 AM | Theater 6

Bosch Power Tools, a global leader in tools and accessories, uses the SAP Sales and Service Cloud application to optimize customer service. With Joule agents, Bosch boosts automation, improves ticket routing, reduces manual work, and accelerates case resolution — driving sales and service growth.

Carlisle: Transforming customer service to drive sustainable growth - CXP2791
Wednesday, May 21 | 1:00 PM | Theater 6

Known for its innovative building products, Carlisle Companies Inc. is transforming service with SAP Service Cloud solutions. The company is enhancing self-services, accelerating case resolution, and improving sales order support, while paving the way for new AI innovations.

Revolutionize customer service with AI agents in SAP Service Cloud - CXP2259
Tuesday, May 20 | 3:00 PM |Theater 13

Connect the SAP Service Cloud solution with the SAP ERP application to optimize data, workforce, and processes while leveraging AI at every stage of issue resolution to boost productivity and outcomes. Discover how SAP Service Cloud automates customer service processes and accelerates resolutions, driving efficiency with AI agents.

Elevating service with SAP Service Cloud solutions (Demo station)- CXP2628
all day | Location: Applications, 135

Accelerate issue resolution in customer service and shared services with SAP Service Cloud and SAP Enterprise Service Management solutions. Boost productivity with AI and transform employee and customer experiences with AI agents. Gain synergies from connectivity with SAP solutions to maximize efficiency across the value chain.

How Joule Agents transform the quote-to-service process - CXP1670
Tuesday, May 20 | 2:30 PM | Theater 13

Tracking orders, checking availability, requesting quotes—these manual tasks consume valuable time and resources. Discover a strategic approach for using the Joule Agents package across the customer journey to save time and reduce costs, enhancing online interactions, automating e-mail processing, and supporting your team with AI-assisted insights.

Start putting together your personal agenda today. Check out the full session catalog.

entry.title: Re: S4 call to F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST returning all values even when selecting just one.
entry.content:
Hello Sandra

I'm still waiting for the access to debug in production but just want to share something that may be the reason behind why it is not working.

On the left S4 on the right ECC, the code has changed and the OCX_OFF is not set in S4 unless mark_tab has something in it.

Will test once I have access to production.

Thank you

entry.title: Create Instance using CLI command for BTP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
The CPI trial comes with a time limit and gets deleted once the time limit is crossed. You'll have to perform all tasks again when you subscribe after deletion. One of the tasks is to create instances.

Let us say you want to create service instance for plan api and integration-flow. Usually we use btp cockpit to create it and it is easy to set up. What if you have to repeat the same, once trial tenant is deleted. Creating instances using commands can be easy and we can run the same command every time we set up the trial account.

Usually we need three instances creation for setting up the trial tenant to get started with development.

  1. Plan api ( access cloud integration artifacts)
  2. Plan integration-flow ( sending message to cpi flow)
  3. Plan apiportal-apiaccess ( accessing APIM artifacts )

I am making use of CLI command for creating the instances. 

Install CLI command

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/download-and-install-cloud-foundry-command-line-interface

Once installed check if it is available

 

or you can make use of SAP business application studio and it comes with installed cf

https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/sap-business-application-studio/terminal

Login

When you log in for the first time, you need to add your api endpoint. You will find it in the BTP cockpit.

Click on the link shown in the terminal. 

login with your user

copy this key and paste it in your terminal

 

My space is dev and all the instances will be created under dev space.

Create instance for plan api

When you create instance we need to provide some parameters and these parameters are kept in json format. Instead of complicating things, First we create a one instance using BTP cockpit ui. Let us call it test_blog and I have assigned one role once create this is how it looks like

Copy the json file and put it in the folder and file name is instance-api.json

Json

{
    "grant-types": [
        "client_credentials"
    ],
    "redirect-uris": [],
    "roles": [
        "AuthGroup_IntegrationDeveloper"
    ],
    "token-validity": 43200
}

Now we will use json as parameter file for creating a new instance.

cf create-service it-rt api odata-api -c instance-api.json
cf create-service-key odata-api key-01

 

Create instance for plan integration-flow

Repeat the same step here as well to get the json file.

json file

{
    "grant-types": [
        "client_credentials"
    ],
    "redirect-uris": [],
    "roles": [
        "ESBMessaging.send"
    ],
    "token-validity": 3600
}

 

Iflow
cf create-service it-rt integration-flow iflow_blog -c instance-integration-flow.json
cf create-service-key iflow_blog key-01
 

You can view the instance and key.

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/specifying-service-instance-and-service-key-parameters-in-json-format

APIM plan apiportal-apiaccess

Json

{
	    "role": "APIPortal.Administrator"
	}

 

cf create-service apimanagement-apiportal-trial apiportal-apiaccess apim_odata -c instance-api-access.json
cf create-service-key apim_odata key-01

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/api-access-plan-for-api-portal

Please be aware sometimes btp trial is giving error when creating this instance be it from cockpit or from terminal. I had a issue and posted here and after sometime it starts working again. 
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/instance-creation-for-apiportal-apiaccess-plan-in-btp-trial-fails/qaq-p/14088211

View Keys

Once you are done with creating keys you can run this command to view the keys.

cf service-key odata-api key-01

Conclusion

Once you are done with settting up the commands, next time trial account is deleleted, you can make use of the commands instead of creating them from btp cockpit.

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now SAP Companion on Web - Guided Tour use...
entry.content: Unfortunately there is no data validation in SAP Companion (for Web) Guided Tour steps. You can use Conditions on Help Topics to provide some degree of validation (by setting the Help Topic to only display based on the field value) but Help Topics are not displayed while the Guided Tour is running.

entry.title: Fixed rows in alv webdynpro
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement where i want to have fixed number of record in ALV, Can you please suggest what is best option. I am using set_visible_row_count but it display fixed rows but there is a vertical scroll bar which further allow to see all records. We have blank table where user wants to enter only 20 records.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Handling ONB2.0 rehires in SailPoint
entry.content: Hi - Sure thing. The business rule is quite simple -> IF Job Information.Event Reason = 'Hire' OR 'Rehire' OR 'SCWK', THEN SET Employment Details.CustomField10 = 'Yes'

entry.title: Re: Intercompany integration scenarios FAQ in Cloud ERP, Public Edition
entry.content:
谢谢！

但是“GCR - S/4HANA Customer Advisory Council(CAC) - Content/Resources/大中华区线上分享/In-depth with S4HANA Cloud...”这个链接无权限访问。

entry.title: Re: Status change log in CJ20N for existing projects
entry.content:
The documentation for the 'Status change documents' checkbox states 'If this indicator is set in the project profile, the system sets the indicator in all WBS elements assigned to a new project from the project definition onwards. Changes to the project profile have no influence on existing projects.' 😕

In very very old versions, an OSS note provided a Z-program to correct similar invalid projects, but use it at your own risk (adapt it to the current version, carefully test it in a sandbox, etc.) Basically it updated JSTO for already created projects and WBS but could lead to database inconsistencies (missing JCDS records)

  • 576996 - Projects created from MAnf: missing status change documents

entry.title: Re: S4 call to F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST returning all values even when selecting just one.
entry.content: Thanks. Good catch. Probably a different Support Package level (abnormal situation).

entry.title: Re: Fixed rows in alv webdynpro
entry.content: Pass only the number of lines you want to display then?

entry.title: Re: Two different DM Custom View Plugins are mistakenly c...
entry.content:
Hi Kevin,

Is it possible to have the same plugin available in the POD Designer twice, so I can have a stable version of my custom plugin in one POD and a test version in another?

Thanks,
Ricardo

entry.title: SUM unable to see SAP_UI SAINT package
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am upgrading an ECC 6.0 system from EHP 0 to EHP 8. I am getting the following screen in SUM:

These are the SAP_UI packages which I have in my download directory, I am posting their sizes too:

188641236 May  9 12:36 K-75401INSAPUI.SAR

524616979 May  9 12:37 K-75402INSAPUI.SAR

236181013 May  9 12:37 K-75403INSAPUI.SAR

234557165 May  9 12:37 K-75404INSAPUI.SAR

220483907 May  9 12:38 K-75405INSAPUI.SAR

241319761 May  9 12:38 K-75406INSAPUI.SAR

223602962 May  9 12:38 K-75407INSAPUI.SAR

183131444 May  9 12:38 K-75408INSAPUI.SAR

219687254 May  9 12:39 K-75409INSAPUI.SAR

220659934 May  9 12:39 K-75410INSAPUI.SAR

244020960 May  9 12:39 K-75411INSAPUI.SAR

237387551 May  9 12:39 K-75412INSAPUI.SAR

223318609 May  9 12:40 K-75413INSAPUI.SAR

245645137 May  9 12:40 K-75414INSAPUI.SAR

253781571 May  9 12:40 K-75415INSAPUI.SAR

279323574 May  9 12:40 K-75416INSAPUI.SAR

756609557 May  9 12:36 K-754BHINSAPUI.SAR

     4455 May  9 10:39 SAP_UI750.SAR

     4941 May  9 10:39 SAP_UI754.SAR

As the last 2 are fairly small, I suppose those are ACPs. Additional info:

1. I have also provided all the EHP 8 upgrade export media
2. I have forgotten to set the permissions of the files in the download directory to 777. Now it doesn't help
3. I have tried to find installation files for SAP_UI in the SAP Marketplace (as I think that those could be called SAINT packages), unfortunately without much success.
4. I found the following SAP note for this case:

2621416 - Error: "The program has not found a matching SAINT package for 'xxx'."

however it didn't help at all 😞

Any help would be very warmly welcomed. as I have run out of ideas.

entry.title: How to rename "Total" in SAC story
entry.content:
Hi,

How do i rename the "Total" in a row to "LOANS AND ADVANCES"

Thanks,

Mayura

entry.title: How to monitor SM13 in SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring, and setup custom alerts?
entry.content:
I want to setup monitoring for SM13 "Update process". How do I set up monitoring for this? It is not in the alerts list in SAP Cloud ALM. 

Second question, in Solution Manager you can create your own "Metrics & Alerts". My question to you is, where do I find this option in CALM?    

For example, within SOLMAN_SETUP you navigate to:

  1. Technical Monitoring
  2. Alerting Framework
  3. Alert Configuration
  4. This is where you:
    • Define alert thresholds
    • Configure notifications
    • Customize alert reactions (e.g., email, incident creation)
    • Adjust monitoring templates for systems, hosts, databases, and interfaces

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to monitor SM13 in SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitorin...
entry.content:
KBA 3426195 has instructions checking what products are supported for monitoring with SAP Cloud ALM, and the available metrics for that product for each use case (e.g. Health Monitoring, Integration & Exception Monitoring, etc.).

SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring provides only specific pre-built metrics for the products it supports. So it is not currently possible to create "custom" metrics to monitor any-and-all aspects of a product. You are limited to monitoring a product using the pre-built metrics Health Monitoring provides. If we examine the Health Monitoring Content page, there are no entries for "SM13" or "update process". Thus, this is not one of the supported Health Monitoring metrics.

The "Alert" concept has changed somewhat when comparing SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Solution Manager. In Solution Manager, the Alert was the core concept. Now, an alert is considered a downstream aspect. 

The first driver are metrics, and their configured threshold values or filter conditions. Downstream, Events are configured to respond to scenarios where threshold values are passed, or specific data is detected (use case dependent). The Events control the Alert creation and notifications. Thus, Alert creation, notifications (e.g. e-mail, chat, etc.), and ticket creation are all driven by "events" in SAP Cloud ALM. 

Some SAP Cloud ALM for Operations use cases allow the creation of very configurable and customizable events. Integration & Exception Monitoring is one of them. However, Health Monitoring does not permit the creation of custom events. Like the metrics in Health Monitoring, you must use the pre-built events for Alert and e-mail creation. If a metric is not supported, it will not have a pre-built event for Alert creation. More information can be found in KBA 3398745.

entry.title: Re: ONB 2.0 Documents Integration to OpenText
entry.content:
Hi @zmuhammad2911,

I also have the same requirement. 

Where you able to send to OpenText those documents that have been electronically signed or generated (without been electronically signed) in the ONB 2.0 process? 

We do have onboardees uploading documents but We also need to integrate to OpenText those documents that are generated (With or without sign) in the ONB2.0 process.

Really appreciate your comments.

Kind regards,

 
@Gopinath  @Manjunath_BK 

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding SAP SuccessFactors Extended Enterprise Content Management by OpenText

entry.title: Re: How to exclude users from IPS sync
entry.content:
Hi

I think you can simply do the filter directly using the sf.user.filter property in the source system (SF).

The names of the attributes are different depending on the api version, so you should check the property sf.api.version.

If you are using v2 check this note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003464278

The default value once you've done your initial sych us usually set to "active eq true" so only active users in SF are synched. Not sure if you have a specific reason to synch based on other attributes...you can add to your filter something like ...and userName ne "xxxx". This is valid for v1, for v1 you should check the attribute names and operands in the documentation.

entry.title: Issue with Data Anonymization Processes DRTM Inactive Application and Candidate Purge
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently facing an issue with our automated data anonymization processes, specifically DRTM Inactive Application Purge and DRTM Inactive Candidate Purge. These processes are designed to anonymize data for candidates who applied more than six months ago.

For the past two months, we have encountered a recurring problem: between the time the purge is executed and the responsible party confirms the anonymization (even if this is only a few hours), some individuals on the anonymization list manage to submit their resumes. As a result, we lose their data.

Could you please advise if there is a way to prevent this issue? We are looking for a solution that ensures no data is lost during this process.

Thank you!

Vera

entry.title: Re: Live stream your in-person event so anyone in the SAP Community can join
entry.content: Great to have this documented for others to use @ajmaradiaga - fantastic contribution, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Intercompany integration scenarios FAQ in Cloud ERP, Public Edition
entry.content:
另外，请问下：

含MTO或VC的高级公司间销售（5D2）以及公司间转储（5HP）在S/4 HANA OP/PCE的哪个版本可以使用呢？

谢谢！

entry.title: Re: SAP APIM external Logging using New Relic
entry.content:
Hi @Muniyappan 

Thank you very much for prompt response!

I am not on Mac and using Windows Machine. As you suggested, I tried zipping the file after downloading it and even without doing any changes, I am getting the error while trying to upload it to APIM. 

APIProxy is the root folder inside the downloaded/zipped folder:

I have tried compressing it with the default Windows utility, 7-zip and powershell, but  it always throws that error while uploading it back in SAP APIM. Any further suggestions?

Thanks again!

entry.title: Re: Control records for actual CCS missing for valuation...
entry.content: you need to execute this "FCML4H_CCS_STARTUP" in SE38 instead of saving it, try again and write here the status.

entry.title: SAP MDG Certificate
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm taking the Learning Journey for SAP MDG (MDG100 and MDG200). I have no background in SAP MDG except for Material Master Data. I took the test and failed.

My question is, are there additional resources out there, besides the virtual classroom, that can help me with the test and better understand the content? Any studying tips? Videos that are according to SAP teaching would help me a lot. I appreciate any help you can provide.

entry.title: Re: DRC France FR_WHT_DAS2 - Occupation field showing no...
entry.content:
Dear Sushma,

 

The DAS2 report enables you to generate your report and fulfill the statutory reporting requirements in France. 

Please make sure you have specify values for parameters specific to the report category in the Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting (ID: 101071) activity. Refer the below help document for your reference,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/33b132c0121c41f3b412258173d76b0d/f79c56d728904b948ca925923b5e50ae.html?locale=en-US

After maintaining the necessary configuration, kindly rerun the France DAS2 Withholding Tax report from the Run Statutory Reports app.

If the values still do not appear in the 'Data Preview' for the 'Occupation' column, please raise an incident with SAP under the DRC (Document and Reporting Compliance) component area

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Wroclaw 2025
entry.content:
CLICK HERE TO REGISTER FOR THE EVENT!

Capgemini, in cooperation with WSB Merito Wrocław University, is organizing another edition of the SAP Inside Track conference in Wrocław, Poland!

This is an unmissable event for anyone working with SAP products, developing in the ABAP language, keeping up with news regarding AI in SAP, SAP BTP or PO, or preparing for SAP Certification.

entry.title: Re: Making forms available to an external company or person
entry.content: Have you found the solution for this ?

entry.title: Disable filters on library search - LMS - SuccessFactors
entry.content:
User has asked to limit the filters on the Library Search for the New Learning Experience, for example to delete the Currency filter as it is not relevant. 

According to this link, it is possible to change the filters however I can't find information about how this is done.

Thank you,

entry.title: User Experience Overviews at SAP Sapphire Orlando & Madrid
entry.content:
Join us in Orlando May 20-21 or Madrid May 27-28 to get an overview of the latest user experience innovations along with an outlook on what is planned.

Figure 1: Alt Text: Image of Arin Bhowmick, Chief Design Officer, SAP, with the title and ID of his talk, along with date and location for Orlando.

Listen to Arin Bhowmick, our Chief Design Officer:

Title: SAP's user-experience strategy to achieve better outcomes

Orlando: PUX2821, May 20, 2025 3:30 – 3:50 PM EDT

Madrid: PUX1497, May 27, 2025, 1:30 – 1:50 PM CEST

Abstract: Understand how SAP is leading the way to help business users get work done easily and with better outcomes. Our strategy provides a consistent and integrated suite-first experience, infuses AI nearly everywhere, makes it easier for our customers and partners to design and build great UX, and leverages our user research framework to meet your needs.

Figure 2: Alt Text: Image of Thomas Reiss, Chief Design Officer, SAP, with the title and ID of his talk, along with date and location for Orlando.

Join me for my road map talk:

Title: User experience innovations in our products: For today and tomorrow

Orlando: PUX2826, May 20, 2025 4:30 – 4:50 PM EDT

Madrid: PUX1498, May 28, 2025, 9:00 – 9:20 AM CEST

Abstract: Have a look at the latest UX innovations across our suite of applications, along with cool innovations coming soon. Examine SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA, as well as cloud products such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Ariba solutions. Also, check out selected highlights from UI technologies.

 

Orlando: Further UX Talks

Let me call out these additional UX specific talks:

  • Suite-first experience: Seamless, consistent, and integrated
    PUX2825 Tuesday, 1:00 pm
  • Creating a great UX for your own applications with SAP Design System PUX2827 Tuesday, 1:30 pm
  • AI on the go: Run your business with SAP Mobile Start and Joule
    PUX2818 Tuesday, 2:00 pm
  • From insights to impact: Elevating product success through your feedback PUX2822 Tuesday, 3:00 pm
  • AI-first user experience: What's in it for you?
    PUX2824 Wednesday, 11:00 am
  • An overview of our AI-fueled mobile products across SAP Business Suite PUX2819 Wednesday, 11:30 am
  • Our mobile experiences empowering today’s and tomorrow’s business users PUX3224 Wednesday, 1:00 pm

Find out more in the session catalog for Sapphire Orlando, May 19-21: Product and User Experience Track.

Beyond that, there are a number of interesting UX-related talks in other tracks, have a look here for a nice summary by Peter Spielvogel:

  • UX, AI, SAP S/4HANA transformation sessions at SAP Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference 2025.

 

Madrid: Further UX Talks

  • Creating a great UX for your own applications with SAP Design System PUX1499 Wednesday, 11:30 pm
  • From insights to impact: Elevating product success through your feedback PUX1495 Tuesday, 11:00 am
  • An overview of our AI-fueled mobile products across SAP Business Suite PUX1494 Wednesday, 3:30 pm

Find out more in the session catalog for Sapphire Madrid, May 26-28: Product and User Experience Track (6 talks).

 

More Information

I hope to see you either in Orlando or Madrid – feel free to approach me and ask questions!

In case you missed my recent series of blog posts about our recent UX innovations, do have a look, it starts with this one, which contains links to all the other six posts in the series:

SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More).

entry.title: Is there a Fiori app to read the data's invoice from a QR?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Fiori gurus

We have the requirement to read a QR of an invoice and save some data.

Is there any standard Firoi app to fulfilled?

entry.title: Re: Function Module in Variant Configuration
entry.content: Hello, I am really interested in managing mare than one possible output in a variant function letting the user select later the right value.

entry.title: Re: Function Module in Variant Configuration
entry.content:
Hello, I am really interested in managing more than one possible output in a variant function letting the user select later the right value. Do you still have any idea how to manage this scenario?

For example, using variant function to get all the BOM items from a header material number (already specified in a charcateristic) and the let the user select through a F4 help which of the possible items will be the selected one. 

Do you see any other work around solution (maybe we do not need to use variant functions)?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Functional area substitution is not working through BADI
entry.content:
Hi bhavan

Please try with the Substitution/Validation for Market Segments

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e6175604fc874bfe84aa14d2f9ae1fbd/928a56280178449f8f25cd7d63b397fb.html

Here is the link of the Deriving Funtional Area that provide more details about the functionality

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e6175604fc874bfe84aa14d2f9ae1fbd/c26bf851a09a3f3ae10000000a441470.html

 

I hope this help you.

Edna

entry.title: Re: SAP Sapphire 2025 in Orlando: Discover the Power of GenAI for Enterprise Transformation
entry.content:
Great to see your first post in the community! And can't wait to read your next post about how the sessions go 😉.

In response to the important stuff: As an SEO, I can confirm: when no one laughs, the AI just quietly rolls out a core update, and the rest of us scramble to figure out what changed.

entry.title: Re: My new UDO does not appear in UDO's list in a UDF wit...
entry.content:
In the end, the solution was to remove the object and recreate it.

I don't have a clue why it didn't work the first time, but thankfully, B1 wa able to recover with a remove-recreate procedure.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Perú - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to run the SAP DRC enabled legal reports for my country. However, the reports are not displaying information and not processing any information the templates. Below, I detail the procedures performed.

1) In the CBC, an organizational structure for data processing has already been created.

2) Activation of the Document and Report Compliance (5XU) scope element has been requested.

3) My user has all the necessary roles for the execution of legal reports.

BR_AP_MANAGER_PE

BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT_PE

BR_ADMINISTRATOR

4) In the app, the reports are displayed.

5) However, in this application it does not allow to run legal reports.

Please support any additional configuration that may be required. ?

that might be required to run SAP DRC legal reports ?

Thanks,

Antony

entry.title: Re: Export multiple datasphere objects at the same time a...
entry.content: The ER will not allow to restore the objects however, but via CLI one can download all objects

entry.title: Re: Notification when GR(Goods Receipt ) created within F...
entry.content:
Hi veeravani,

We have a business event (https://api.sap.com/event/CE_MATERIALDOCUMENTEVENTS/overview) that could be consumed in Event Mesh for example and then send a notification to the user.

You might need to check out Developer Extensibility and create an Event consumption model.

The other idea would be to check out Situation Handling https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/516c8ecb7462453ca430a42481cc33ec.html?locale=en-US

Thank you

Jerry
